sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 53:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section II, List of Answers on Topics 53:1
items.html: Because coarseness is the vessel of drawing, and the primary vessel of reception, through the reflected light that rises from it. For this reason, the coarseness in the vessel is regarded as its internality. The lesser the coarseness, the more external it is considered, and that which is completely refined in it is regarded as completely external (See answer 55). (Inner Observation, Chapter One, Item 5)
